response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished web and application software developer with a strong track record in full-stack, low-latency, and distributed systems work, but the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep hardware-level expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design that is entirely absent from his profile. His most performance-relevant experience (low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby, async I/O with DLang) operates firmly in the software domain, and his recent AI-adjacent work involves agentic software tooling rather than deep-learning workload analysis or accelerator firmware. There is no evidence of SIMD/MIMD architecture experience, neural network training/inference optimization, or any hardware modeling background.
strengths: 14+ years of professional experience satisfies the BS+8yr educational path requirement
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering in high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency caching)
Broad polyglot background including embedded and systems-level work in C/C++ and DLang
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience whatsoever — the core technical requirement of the role is completely unmet
No GPU architecture, AI accelerator, or deep-learning workload optimization background; AI exposure is limited to agentic software frameworks
Skillset is exclusively web/application layer with no hardware-software co-design or chip-level performance analysis experience
